member.name: Manuela Perteghella
member.synopsis: Manuela Perteghella is the Liberal Democrat MP for Stratford-on-Avon , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Money Laundering Regulations (MLRs), businesses undertaking regulated activity, including facilitating property transactions, must establish policies, controls and procedures to mitigate the risks of money laundering and terrorist financing. The...

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that the Modern Service Framework for Frailty and Dementia establishes care co-ordination as a minimum national expectation for people living with dementia, and if
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are engaging with a wide group of partners to understand what should be included in the modern service framework to ensure the best outcomes for people living with dementia. We continue to engage key professional groups, including people with lived exp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dementia: Palliative Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to ensure that the Modern Service Framework for Palliative and End of Life Care recognises dementia as a life-limiting condition that requires a constant palliative approach from d
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that people living with dementia can have significant palliative care and end-of-life care needs. We are working closely with a range of stakeholders, including Dementia UK, on the development of a Modern Service Framework (MSF) ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: Digital waste tracking is one of the major structural reforms being implemented under the Waste Crime Action Plan to design waste crime out of the system and will apply to waste operators in all areas of the UK. Defra is leading the delivery of the Action...

writtenQuestions.heading: Waste: Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what weight is given to environmental harm when determining penalties for waste crime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The sentencing guidelines for waste crime offences take into account environmental harm, for example, if there is polluting material of a dangerous nature and if there will be any effect on air or water quality, amenity value or animal health or flora. Th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is taking a firm, zero‑tolerance approach to waste crime, ensuring penalties reflect the harm caused. The Waste Crime Action Plan commits Defra to working with the Ministry of Justice to review whether existing penalties, including fines, a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Waste Disposal: Monitoring

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Environment Agency (EA) is working with their internal compliance and enforcement teams and external partners to ensure we maximise the potential of Digital Waste Tracking to tackle waste crime. They are developing a range of measures that cover preve...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answerText: The new steel trade measure has been designed to only cover steel requirements that can be met in the UK. Where this is not feasible for technical reasons, for example where single product codes contain different sizes or grades of steel products, quota s...

writtenQuestions.answerText: In June 2025, Government introduced new national standards, making clear how sustainable drainage systems (SuDS) should be designed and maintained. Standard 6 dictates SuDS should maximise biodiversity benefits. Defra has not made a central estimate of wi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Conveyancing: Reform

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the Home Buying and Selling Reform Roadmap which my Department published on 19 June. It can be found on gov.uk here.

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to ensure that future children’s online safety policy reflects the experiences of children with additional needs.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to help ensure that parents and carers have access to clear, accessible and tailored support to help them manage children’s online safety.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises the vital role parents and carers play in supporting children’s online safety. We launched the 'You Won’t Know Until You Ask’ campaign and the Kids Online Safety Hub to provide them with practical tools to build children’s resili...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Media: Fraud

registeredInterests.category: 2. (b) Any other support not included in Category 2(a)

items.text: Name of donor: Crowne Plaza Hotel Stratford-upon-Avon (part of IHG Hotels and Resorts group) Address of donor: Bridgefoot, Stratford-upon-Avon Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Hosting a Hospitality and Tourism forum for local businesses and arts venues in my constituency of Stratford-upon-Avon. This event was co-organised by my office and the Crowne Plaza Hotel Managers and the value provided for this event includes the hire of the room and catering for attendees, value £7,596.50 Date received: 2 March 2026 Date accepted: 2 March 2026 Donor status: company, registration 03130330 (this is the IHG company number) (Registered 12 March 2026)
Trustee, 1st Welford on Avon Scout Group. This is an unpaid role. (Registered 1 August 2024)

Member of University College London Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust. This is an unpaid role. (Registered 1 August 2024)
